description: RGB shows the Lahaina coastal town with a large dark brown/grey area inland matching the burned district, clearly distinct from surrounding dry reddish terrain. SWIR confirms an extensive burn scar as a dark brown mass, with no active fire glow — consistent with post-fire aftermath roughly one week after peak.
labeler_notes: Burn scar footprint is large and unambiguous in both views. No active fire signal in SWIR — aftermath only, so HIGH not CRITICAL.

description: RGB shows dry upland agricultural and scrub terrain with cloud cover obscuring the right half of the frame; no smoke, burn scar, or fire signal visible in the exposed areas. SWIR shows bright green healthy vegetation on the cloud-free left portion and orange/tan tones for dry scrub — no hazard signature.
labeler_notes: Tile is offset from the Lahaina burn area; right-half cloud cover limits interpretation but the exposed terrain shows no hazard.

description: RGB shows the same Lahaina coastal scene as the Aug 17 capture; the burned town area remains visible as a distinctive dark brownish-grey patch along the coastal strip, contrasting with surrounding dry land. SWIR confirms the persistent burn scar as a dark brown inland area with healthy vegetation signatures only at the margins.
labeler_notes: Burn scar still clearly visible three weeks post-peak; useful as a confirmed aftermath example. Very similar to o0 Aug 17 tile.

description: RGB shows upland mixed agricultural and scrub terrain with partial cloud cover on the right side; the exposed left portion is brown/dry but shows no burn scar or smoke. SWIR displays bright green (healthy vegetation) on the right and orange/tan (dry scrub) on the left with no burn or fire signal.
labeler_notes: Consistent with the Aug 17 o1 tile — offset from burn area, cloud-partially obscured, no hazard visible.

description: RGB shows Kelowna city on the west shore of Okanagan Lake under clear skies with no smoke, fire, or anomaly visible. SWIR shows healthy bright green vegetation in residential and park areas with urban magenta/pink tones — entirely normal urban scene, no hazard evidence.
labeler_notes: Pre-peak capture (event peak Aug 18); clean baseline urban scene.

description: RGB shows a hillside residential and forested area north of Kelowna under clear skies; terrain is mixed green and brown (typical summer dry conditions) with no smoke or burn scar. SWIR shows a mix of healthy green vegetation and orange/tan tones indicating dry but unstressed scrub — no fire signal present.
labeler_notes: Pre-peak capture; orange SWIR tones reflect dry summer conditions not a hazard. Worth noting as a pre-event baseline for the hillside tile.

description: RGB shows the Kelowna city lakefront on the peak fire date with clear skies and no visible smoke or burn in this tile; the urban grid and waterfront are intact. SWIR confirms healthy green vegetation throughout with normal urban signatures — no fire or smoke encroachment visible in this particular offset.
labeler_notes: Fire peak date but this city-center tile shows no hazard; smoke/fire activity was to the north of this footprint (visible in o1).

description: RGB shows a dense smoke plume billowing over the hillside north of Kelowna with a visible dark burn scar on the terrain below the smoke; the hillside shows striated brown burn patterns cutting through residential streets. SWIR shows orange/tan burn-affected vegetation across the hillside and a bright region beneath the smoke consistent with an active or very recent fire front.
labeler_notes: Smoke partially obscures the burn area but active fire evidence is strong enough for CRITICAL. This is the standout high-value capture in the batch — peak fire day, active smoke plume, confirmed burn on hillside.

description: RGB is almost entirely obscured by thick cloud cover; only a small fragment of terrain is visible in the lower-left corner. SWIR likewise shows a blue-green cloud signal with no usable ground information.
labeler_notes: Greater than 90% cloud cover — ground is not interpretable.

description: RGB is essentially a white-out — dense cloud fills over 95% of the frame with only a tiny sliver of terrain at the far right edge. SWIR is uniformly cyan/cloud throughout with no ground signal.
labeler_notes: Completely cloud-obscured; no useful information can be extracted.
